meaning: n.流走之物,决赛,决定性竞选
examples: Water absorption is greatly reduced; consequently runoff is increased, resulting in accelerated erosion rates.
Recent studies of ancient Maya water management have found that the urban architecture of some cities was used to divert rainfall runoff into gravity-fed systems of interconnected reservoirs.
There will be a runoff between these two candidates on December 9th.
That's 100 times the normal count found in water runoff from storms, the company said.
If no candidate wins an absolute majority, a runoff will be held Aug. 11.
